message:
feat(core,abi): port lossy UTF-8 decoder to core::string::wchar (bd-utf-epic)

wchar_abi.rs::decode_utf8 was a 52-line replacement-char-on-invalid
UTF-8 decoder (returns 0xFFFD + advance count rather than None).
core::string::wchar::mbtowc has the strict reject-on-invalid variant.
Added a lossy companion to core so both semantics live together —
used by display / display-width / printf-render paths that need to
walk a buffer without aborting on garbage.

New core surface (in string::wchar):
  pub const REPLACEMENT_CODEPOINT: u32 = 0xFFFD;
  pub fn decode_utf8_lossy(bytes: &[u8]) -> (u32, usize);

The decoder validates: continuation-without-lead, RFC 3629 forbidden
lead bytes (0xF8..=0xFF), overlong 2/3/4-byte encodings, surrogates
(U+D800..=U+DFFF), above-max (U+10FFFF), truncated sequences. The
"advance past the malformed sequence" guarantee — every call returns
n_consumed >= 1 — lets callers walk a buffer end-to-end via
`i += n` without getting stuck on garbage.

abi changes:
  - Drop inline decode_utf8 (~52 lines).
  - `use ... decode_utf8_lossy as decode_utf8;` keeps the 2 call sites
    (display-width + printf-render paths) byte-identical.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-utf-2):
  - 16 new core::string::wchar lossy tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 109/109 wchar_abi + conformance_diff_wchar integration tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-utf-epic.
